form_answers.question: Is ‘Other goods & services that increase independence’ approved in the budget?
Is ‘Other goods & services that increase health and safety’ approved in the budget?
Is the item related to a need or goal identified in the Life Plan?
Will the item increase the independence and/or health and safety of the individual?
Is the item exclusively needed for the participant?
Is the item on the OTPS exclusion list? (see guidelines)
Can the item be funded through other sources?
Was the same item bought already in this budget year?

request.justification: A weighted blanket helps Shira with sensory regulation and sleep, supporting her health and safety at home.
request.extraction_notes: Safety features: Breathable fabric; evenly distributed weight; sized to the individual No 'Name of Provider/Vendor' label on this form; the provider/vendor name could not be read (the item link points to 'amazon.com').

findings.criterion_text: The item exists at the linked page, with a visible price
findings.note: Could not confirm the item and price on the page — please review the listing.

findings.criterion_text: Item is plausibly NOT on the OTPS exclusion list (not cable TV, common household supplies, rental cars, legal fees, co-pays, experimental therapies)
findings.note: The requested item does not match anything on the exclusion list.

findings.criterion_text: Fee-match: listed price is consistent with the price on the form, and within the $3,000/budget-year cap
findings.note: Could not parse a fee from both the form and the website — please compare manually.

findings.criterion_text: OTPS is approved in the participant's budget
findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

findings.criterion_text: Item supports a goal in the participant's Life Plan
findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

summary: Automated rule-based check of the provider's website found 1 of 3 website-verifiable items, with 2 needing manual review. No AI model was used for this review — a human should double-check any 'Needs Review' items before deciding.
